extra.reasoning:
The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" is violated because transition t16 sets sent:=true while opted_out is true, provided stage>=4 (i.e., stage==4, since stage is in [0,4]).

Reachability of stage==4 with opted_out true:
- t2 (the only other sent:=true transition) requires NOT opted_out, so it is safe.
- t16 fires on send_attempt when opted_out AND stage>=4. Stage 4 is reachable via the chain: stage0--key_1-->stage1 (t3), stage1--key_0-->stage2 (t6), stage2--key_0-->stage3 (t9), stage3--key_1-->stage4 (t12). None of these touch opted_out (it stays false; they set sent:=false).

Concrete 6-event execution (within the 7-event bound):
1. key_1 (t3, stage==0): -> opted_out=false, sent=false, stage=1
2. key_0 (t6, stage==1): -> opted_out=false, sent=false, stage=2
3. key_0 (t9, stage==2): -> opted_out=false, sent=false, stage=3
4. key_1 (t12, stage==3): -> opted_out=false, sent=false, stage=4
5. optout (t1): -> opted_out=true, sent=false, stage=4
6. send_attempt (t16, opted_out and stage>=4): -> opted_out=true, sent=true, stage=4

Final state has opted_out=true and sent=true, so opted_out implies not sent fails. Property VIOLATED.
